titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Masood Ali Wilbert Warren Papers
titlestmt.author: Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 7293190.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday February 11, 2021 at 04:08

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Masood Ali Wilbert Warren Papers includes sketchbooks, sketches, and drawings created by Warren, 1933-1988, as well as photographs and collected material.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

bioghist.head: Masood Ali Wilbert Warren (1907-1995)
bioghist.p: Masood Ali Wilbert Warren was an African American artist. He was born in Notasulga, Alabama, of African and American Indian heritage. Warren attended the Art Students League in New York from 1932 to 1935 and earned a Bachelor of Fine Arts in mural painting from New York University in 1939. He also served in the United States Army as a staff artist in the Special Services and the Quartermaster Corps during and after World War II, 1941-1950. After living briefly in Los Angeles, California, Warren was recalled into active service by the United States Air Force during the Korean War as a combat artist serving from 1953 to 1955. He returned to Los Angeles and then completed a Master of Fine Arts with a major in sculpture at Temple University in 1961. His work exhibited at the National Academy of Design and National Art Club.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Masood Ali Wilbert Warren Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Waiting for Godot Books on the James Weldon Johnson Memorial Collection of Negro Arts & Letters Fund, 2006.

Purchased from William Reese Co. (Swann Galleries', New York, sales, including: sale 2189, 8 Oct 2009, lot 26 and lot 27, on the James Weldon Johnson Memorial Collection of Negro Arts & Letters Fund, 2009.

Purchased from William Reese Co. (Swann Galleries', New York, sales, including: sale 2204, 2010 Feb 25, lot 137; sale 2293, 2011 Mar 10, lot 158, lot 159 and lot 160; sale 2408, 2016 March 31, lot 143) on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2009-2011
Purchased from William Reese Co. (Swann Galleries', New York, sale 2342, 2014 March 27) on the Alfred Z. Baker, Jr. Fund, 2014.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Masood Ali Wilbert Warren Papers. James Weldon Johnson Memorial Collection in the Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Masood Ali Wilbert Warren Papers includes sketchbooks, sketches, and drawings created by Warren, 1933-1988. It chiefly consists of approximately 4,450 sketches from life, including street portraits in Harlem, New York City, 1933-1940 and 1968-1988, and of military personnel, 1941-1943 and 1953-1956, as well as 74 drawings of military personnel and others from 1953 to 1956. The collection also includes photographs of Warren alone and with others as well as with examples of his artwork, 1941-1965, and a small group of collected material.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This material is open for research. Early descriptions of the collection, 2006-2007, identified only seven sketchbooks with a collection title, "Masood Ali Wilbert Warren, Sketches of African American Women and Men." In 2020, accessions acquired since 2007 combined with the seven sketchbooks to form the " Masood Ali Wilbert Warren Papers."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sketchbooks and sketches from life by Warren including street portraits in Harlem, New York City, 1933-1940, and of military personnel, 1941-1943 and 1953-1956. This component consists of 48 sketchbooks or portions of sketchbooks and 27 groups of sketches that combine to encompass approximately 4,450 sketches.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with a red back board and the manuscript title, "Fine Arts, Life Drawings" on the pastedown, and lacking the front board, consists of 158 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women in seated and standing poses as well as representations of animals including cats, ducks, parrots, and swans.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with tan boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Life Drawing and Sketches" and "Vol. 5" on the front pastedown sheet, consists of 90 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women in seated and standing poses as well as representations of birds and cityscapes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with mauve boards and the manuscript title, "Sketches, Anatomy Studies, Animal, Nature" on the front flyleaf and "Vol. 8" on the front pastedown, consists of 135 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men, including African American baseball players, and women in seated and standing poses as well as representations of animals including ducks, geese, and storks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with blue boards with imprinted title, "Notes," and the manuscript title, "Life Sketches" on the front flyleaf and "Vol. 12" on the front pastedown, consists of 121 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men, including Japanese Americans, and women in seated and standing poses as well as representations of animals including ducks, geese, a lion, and squirrels.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with blue boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Book, A Sketch of Ears 'de vive,' Life Sketches, Illustrating the Fine Arts" on the front flyleaf and "Vol. 9" on the front pastedown, consists of 110 pencil sketches. Images include details of ears created on October 16, 1934, and remaining sketches created June 9-28, 1935?, of figure sketches of men and women in seated and standing poses.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with light blue boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and manuscript title, "Life Sketches" on the front flyleaf and "Vol. 14 on the front pastedown, consists of 127 pencil sketches. Images chiefly consist of figure sketches of men and women, as well as occasional images of children and representations of birds, American bison, dogs, and squirrels. Includes a manuscript summary description of Elizabeth Jennings Graham (1827-1901) and Jennings v. Third Ave. Railroad (1855), which led to the desegregation of the Third Avenue Railroad Company in New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with light blue boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Life Sketches, Fine Arts" and "Vol. 17" on the front pastedown, consists of 120 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women in seated and standing poses.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with red boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Life Sketches, Nature" on the front flyleaf, consists of 115 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women in seated and standing poses as well as representations of a horse and a landscape.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with green boards with imprinted title, "The Scribbldor," and the manuscript title, "Quick Sketches, 'Artistic Beauty,' Realism" on the front flyleaf and "Vol. 10" on the front pastedown, consists of 146 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women in seated and standing poses as well as representations of dogs, horses, and landscapes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with green boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Sketches from Life" on the front flyleaf and "Vol. 21" on the front pastedown, consists of 110 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women in seated and standing poses as well as representations of lions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with green boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Fine Arts" and "Vol. 22" on the front pastedown, consists of 108 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women in seated and standing poses. Includes a portrait of Fats Waller performing at the Apollo Theater at Harlem, Manhattan, New York City, on June 3, 1936.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with blue boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Fine Arts" on the front flyleaf and "Vol. 23" on the front pastedown, consists of 108 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women in seated and standing poses as well as a view of Columbus Circle, New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with light blue boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Life Sketches" on the front flyleaf, consists of 108 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women in seated and standing poses as well as a representation of a horse.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with red boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Nature, Life Sketches" on the front flyleaf, consists of 107 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women in seated and standing poses.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with blue boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Life Sketches" on the front flyleaf, consists of 109 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men, women, and children in seated and standing poses as well as representations of bears, hippopotamus, and waterfowl.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with light blue boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Life Sketches" on the front flyleaf, consists of 110 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women in seated and standing poses.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with blue boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Life Sketches" on the front flyleaf, consists of 109 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women in seated and standing poses as well as representations of horses.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with red boards and the manuscript title, "Life Sketches," on the flyleaf consists of 108 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of African American children, women, and men in seated and standing poses, as well as musicians playing violin, upright bass, flute, and piano.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with green boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Sketches from Life" on the front flyleaf, consists of 105 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women in seated and standing poses. Includes a portrait of picketer against the New Far East Service during a labor action by teamsters and longshoreman supported by the American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with orange boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and manuscript title, "Life Sketches" on the front flyleaf, consists of 110 pencil sketches. Images chiefly consist of figure sketches of African American men and women, as well as occasional images of children, white men, and representations of an American bison, a cat, and a tiger.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with red boards with imprinted title, "Recipes," and the manuscript title, "Life Sketches" on the front pastedown sheet, consists of 127 pencil sketches. Images of American bison, bears, cheetahs, a giraffe, lions, a peacock, and tigers as well as landscapes, likely at Central Park, New York City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with orange boards and the manuscript title, "Sketches from Life," on the flyleaf consists of 113 crayon sketches. Images include figure sketches of African American women and men in seated and standing poses, as well as men playing tennis and operating a jackhammer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with green boards and the manuscript title, "Life Sketches," on the flyleaf consists of 109 crayon sketches. Images include figure sketches of African American children, women, and men in seated and standing poses, as well as women on a picket line and a man sleeping.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with purple boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Life Sketches" on the front flyleaf, consists of 107 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women in seated and standing poses as well as representations of an American bison, dogs, lions, and a tiger.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with red boards and the manuscript title, "Life Sketches," on the flyleaf consists of 106 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of African American children, women, and men in seated and standing poses, as well as a man sleeping and a Chinese man. Sketches of animals in the volume depict dogs, cats, a tiger, and a horse.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with brown boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and manuscript title, "Sketches from Life" on the front flyleaf, consists of 97 pencil sketches. Images chiefly consist of figure sketches of African American men and women, as well as occasional images of children and white men. Also representations of dogs and monkeys. Volume lacks three pages.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with green boards and the manuscript title, "Life Sketches" on the flyleaf consists of 124 pencil sketches. Images include figure drawings of African American women and men in seated and standing poses, as well as representations of a police horse and a dog. Numerous drawings depict men wearing uniforms. Several images show a man operating a jackhammer. A laid in sheet of paper includes several phrases in phonetic Arabic that probably correspond to duas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with green boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and manuscript title, "Life Sketches" on the front flyleaf, consists of 126 pencil sketches. Images chiefly consist of figure sketches of African American men and women, as well as occasional images of children, white men, and representations of lions. Volume includes the signature of boxer Joe Louis with a profile portrait created on December 18, 1940.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with blue boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and manuscript titles, "Sketches from Life" on the front flyleaf and "U.S. Army Sketches" on front pastedown, consists of 117 pencil sketches. Images chiefly consist of figure sketches of African American male soldiers, some depicted playing musical instruments or baseball, as well as a representation of a lion.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with black boards and the manuscript title, "Sketches from Life, Fine Arts" on the flyleaf consists of 116 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of African American women and men in seated and standing poses, as well as representations of a dog. Many sketches depict men wearing army uniforms, including soldiers with rifles. Several images show men operating typewriters. A sketch of a standing woman from behind includes the title, "Memory."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with black boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Sketches from Life" on the front flyleaf, consists of 107 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of African American men, chiefly members of the United States Army, in seated and standing poses as well as a representation of a woman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Leaves from a paper pad with perforation on the top of the pages that consist of five pencil sketches of African American men, chiefly members of the United States Army. Formerly laid in disbound volume with blue boards with dates June 13-July 12, 1942?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with red boards and the manuscript title, "Soldier, Life Sketches, Fine Arts" on the flyleaf consists of 122 pencil sketches, although the flyleaf and first page are stamped June 19, 1942. Images include figure sketches of primarily African American men and a few women in seated and standing poses, as well as representations of male and female lions. Numerous sketches depict men wearing army uniforms, including soldiers with rifles. Several images show men operating typewriters. Two sketches of a soldier with a rifle bear the location of Saint Joseph, Missouri, and a sketch of a caboose bears the location of Red Cloud, Nebraska. A manusciprt note on May 3, 1942, states that he "missed sketch for the day - [for the first time] since Dec. 1937."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Disbound volume with black boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and the manuscript title, "Life Sketches" on the front flyleaf, consists of 77 pencil sketches. Pages in the boards may not have been originally bound within the volume. Images include figure sketches of African American men, chiefly members of the United States Army and baseball players, as well as African American women, in seated and standing poses. Sketches with identified places include Oregon and a prison at Walla Walla, Washington, likely the Washington State Penitentiary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Leaves from a paper pad that consist of 36 pencil sketches. Images include figure sketches of African American men, chiefly members of the United States Army and baseball players, as well as African American women, in seated and standing poses. Also includes views of trucks and a jeep. Formerly laid in disbound volume with blue boards and with dates June 13-July 12, 1942?

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with red boards with imprinted title, "The Scribble-in Book," and manuscript title, "Life Sketches," on the front flyleaf, consists of 123 pencil sketches. Images chiefly consist of figure sketches of African American male soldiers, some depicted playing baseball and musical instruments, as well as a few of African American women and representations of lions, probably at the San Diego Zoo, Balboa Park, San Diego, California.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with brown boards with imprinted title, "Write it Down," and manuscript titles, "Sketches from Life" on the front flyleaf and "AAB, Santa Maria, Calif.," which refers to the Santa Maria Army Airfield at Santa Maria, California, on front pastedown , consists of 78 pencil sketches with a few also enhanced by ink, August 5-September 8, 1943. Images chiefly consist of figure sketches of African American male soldiers, some depicted playing baseball, as well as a few of African American women and representations of lions at the San Diego Zoo, Balboa Park, San Diego, California. Also laid in material including a promotion card for an event at the Moorish National Islamic Center, New York City, on August 4, 1940, clipping from the New Amsterdam News , related to Rosh Hashanah services by African American Jews in New York City in 1940, and a "Chemical Warfare Service Pocket Reference Card" printed by Air Service Command in April 1943, as well as an undated ticket from a scale at a F. W. Woolworth Company Store.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with tan boards and manuscript titles on front cover, "Sketches," and on front flyleaf, "Fine Arts BFA," consists of 74 ink sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women personnel in the United States Air Force, United States Army, and United States Navy likely captured at a recreation hall at Fuchu Air Station at Fuchū, Tokyo, Japan. Portraits at sites in the United States, January 13-29, 1954, include Seattle in Washington, San Francisco and Hamilton Army Airfield in California, Omaha in Nebraska, Willow Run and Detroit in Michigan, and New York City. Also includes depictions of barracks tent at the Fuchu Air Station. as well as an autograph of radio personality Bud Collyer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with tan boards manufactured by Apollo and the manuscript title on front flyleaf, "Sketches from Life '55," consists of 66 ink sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women personnel in the United States Air Force, United States Army, and United States Navy likely captured at a recreation hall at a military installation in Japan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Spiralbound volume with brown boards and printed title, "Drawing Block," manufactured by Maruman that consists of 15 ink sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women military personnel and civilians likely captured at a recreation hall at a military installation in Japan. Also includes depictions of a lion.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volume with tan boards manufactured by Apollo consists of 63 ink sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women personnel in the United States Air Force and United States Army likely captured at a recreation hall at a military installation in Japan, as well as discrete depictions of an automobile, a lion, and a chair.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pages from a disbound sketchbook that consists of 17 leaves with 21 ink sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women military personnel and civilians likely captured at a recreation hall at a military installation in Japan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pages from a disbound sketchbook or multiple sketchbooks that consists of 41 leaves with 43 ink sketches. Images include figure sketches of men and women military personnel in the United States Air Force, United States Army, and United States Navy as well as civilians likely captured at a recreation hall at a military installation in Japan. Includes a flyleaf with the manuscript title, "Artiste, Life Sketches by Ali, 1932-1956."

did.physdesc: 36 ink sketches, 31 x 23 centimeters, in 6 folders

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drawings by Warren chiefly portraits of military personnel, 1953-1956, as well as occasional civilians and a view of New York City.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized into three subseries: Identified Subjects, Unidentified Subjects, and Unidentified Subjects with Multiple Drawings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by sitter's surname followed by a depiction of New York City .

did.unittitle: Ahmed, Hamid, Pakistan Navy, PNS Sind
Bonner, Lawrence, United States Air Force
Burr, Derl Elza, United States Navy, USS Rich
Curtis, Robert L., United States Air Force
did.physdesc: 1 ink drawing, 47 x 31 centimeters, in mat, 56 x 38 centimeters

did.unittitle: Dennis, John, Royal Canadian Army Medical Corps
Duran, Arthur, United States Air Force

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscription of forename partially missing.

did.unittitle: Gampon, Frank, United States Coast Guard
Garcia, Adolph, United States Navy, USS Leyte
Guenther, F. J., United States Military Academy
Hedeen, Lee, United States Coast Guard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscription of forename partially missing.

did.unittitle: Kim, S. K., Republic of Korea Marine Corps
Kipen, Sally, United States Air Force
Ludvigsen, Bent, Denmark Søværnet, KDM Alssund
Malone, Daniel, Junior Reserve Officers' Training Corps
Miller, Alfred R., United States Air Force
did.physdesc: 1 ink drawing, 61 x 46 centimeters, in mat, 71 x 57 centimeters

did.unittitle: Okray, Eugene, United States Coast Guard, Cutter Mackinac

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: No forename in inscription.

did.unittitle: Preciliano, Benavides, United States Air Force
did.physdesc: 1 ink drawing, 59 x 46 centimeters, in mat, 72 x 57 centimeters

did.unittitle: Solomon, A.J., United States Air Force
did.physdesc: 1 ink drawing, 61 x 33 centimeters, in mat, 71 x 57 centimeters

did.unittitle: Swink, Ronald, United States Air Force
Wadsworth, William, Technical Sergeant, United States Air Force
did.physdesc: 1 ink and crayon drawing, 46 x 31 centimeters, in mat, 55 x 39 centimeters

did.unittitle: Walters, Joseph, United States Navy, USS Amphion

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged with single subjects alphabetically followed by multiple subjects alphabetically.

did.unittitle: Airman, United States Air Force, bust portrait
Airman, United States Air Force, posed sitting and reading
Airman, United States Air Force, posed sitting and sleeping
Airman, United States Air Force, posed standing
Cadet, Junior Reserve Officers' Training Corps
First sergeant, United States Army or United States Marine Corps
Man wearing a garrison hat with a cockade
Man wearing a patrol cap and eyeglasses, bust portrait

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From pad with a spiral binding.

did.physdesc: 1 ink and crayon drawing, 45 x 26 centimeters, on mount, 56 x 36 centimeters
1 ink and crayon drawing, 46 x 23 centimeters, on mount, 57 x 35 centimeters

did.unittitle: Serviceman, likely in the United States Army
did.physdesc: 1 charcoal and chalk drawing, 30 x 23 centimeters, on mount, 34 x 26 centimeters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed "Ali."

did.unittitle: Serviceman, United States Army or United States Marine Corps, bust portrait
Serviceman, United States Army or United States Marine Corps, posed standing
Two airmen, United States Air Force, posed sitting and sleeping

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Duffle bag of an airman reads "Jerry Cain"

did.unittitle: Two servicemen enlisted in the United States Air Force and United States Navy writing at a table at a United Service Organizations facility
did.physdesc: 1 ink drawing, 31 x 44 centimeters, on mount, 38 x 59 centimeters

did.unittitle: Five sergeants, United States Air Force
did.physdesc: 1 ink drawing, 46 x 62 centimeters, in mat, 49 x 65 centimeters

did.unittitle: Four servicemen, likely in the United States Army, sitting and standing at a bar

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From pad with a spiral binding.

did.unittitle: Three servicemen and a civilian playing cards at a United Service Organizations facility
did.physdesc: 1 ink and crayon drawing, 31 x 46 centimeters, on mount, 41 x 56 centimeters

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged sequentially by quantity of drawings.

did.unittitle: Two group portraits of men playing cards, each group consists of four players, with one group comprised of three sailors and a civilian as well as a serviceman watching
did.physdesc: 2 ink drawings, 31 x 46 centimeters and smaller, on mount, 71 x 56 centimeters

did.unittitle: Two portraits of servicemen, including a Marine in the United States Marine Corps
did.physdesc: 2 ink drawings, 61 x 27 centimeters and smaller, on mount, 71 x 56 centimeters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Middle of one drawing not extant.

did.unittitle: Two portraits of sailors sleeping, United States Navy
did.physdesc: 2 ink drawings, 46 x 31 centimeters and smaller, on mount, 54 x 71 centimeters

did.unittitle: Two portraits of sailors, United States Navy
did.physdesc: 2 ink drawings, 47 x 26 centimeters and smaller, on mount, 71 x 54 centimeters

did.unittitle: Three portraits of men, including a United States Air Force airman sleeping as well as another man sleeping and a man posed standing in underwear
did.physdesc: 3 ink drawings, 22 x 48 centimeters and smaller, on mount, 57 x 72 centimeters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Remnants of a fourth drawing on mount.

did.unittitle: Three portraits of sailors, United States Navy
did.physdesc: 3 ink drawings, 46 x 26 centimeters and smaller, on mount, 56 x 72 centimeters

did.unittitle: Three portraits of sailors, United States Navy, which include one sailor sleeping
did.physdesc: 3 ink drawings, 48 x 23 centimeters and smaller, on mount, 54 x 71 centimeters

did.unittitle: Three portraits of servicemen in the United States Air Force, United States Army, and United States Marine Corps
did.physdesc: 3 ink drawings, 47 x 18 centimeters and smaller, on mount, 56 x 71 centimeters

did.unittitle: Five portraits of servicemen in the United States Air Force, United States Army, United States Coast Guard, United States Marine Corps, and United States Navy
did.physdesc: 5 ink drawings, 46 x 18 centimeters and smaller, on mount, 57 x 72 centimeters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portrait of sailor in the United States Navy missing a large portion.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of Warren alone and with others as well as with examples of his artwork, 1941-1965.

did.unittitle: Masood Ali Wilbert Warren with a head sculpture of an African American soldier as well as the sitter
Masood Ali Wilbert Warren in United States Air Force uniform
Masood Ali Wilbert Warren in Japan and Korea
did.physdesc: 28 photographic prints, 9 x 15 centimeters and smaller

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of Warren alone and with others during his service as a combat Artist for the United States Air Force during the Korean War. Many images were likely created in the vicinity of Fuchu Air Station at Fuchū, Tokyo.

did.unittitle: Masood Ali Wilbert Warren with a sculpture
Masood Ali Wilbert Warren at New York City
Sculpture by Masood Ali Wilbert Warren

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Images include representations of Martin Luther King, Jr. (1929-1968) and Thomas B. McGuire (1920-1945) as well as an African American construction worker and a clergyman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material and ephemera collected by Warren.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advertising cards for religious groups including the New York Baha'i Assembly, the New York Islamic Center, and the Moorish National Islamic Center, as well as a booklet for the Baha'i House of Worship. Also advertising cards for Ja

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper clipping on subjects including Marian Anderson, Sigmund Freud, Hawaii, Japanese American actress "Kimi," Korea, and the Seminole Indians.

did.unittitle: Commemorative stamp of Martin Luther King, Jr., and John F. Kennedy distributed by Liberia
did.physdesc: 1 stamp in single sheet, 8 x 13 centimeters

did.unittitle: Envelope sent to Warren by Madeline Ryan Bijur
did.physdesc: 3 pencil drawings on an envelope, 14 x 10 centimeters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sketches on envelope of a man, bust portrait, as well as woman's legs and a steam shovel
